feed.title: Pronounce — recent dictionary additions
feed.subtitle: How engineers actually pronounce project / product / jargon names. Community-maintained.

entry.title: bcrypt — B crypt
entry.summary: bcrypt is pronounced "B crypt" (/ˈbiː krɪpt/). "B-crypt" — B for Blowfish + crypt.

entry.title: EC2 — E two C
entry.summary: EC2 is pronounced "E two C" (/ˌiː tuː ˈsiː/). "E-two-C". Elastic Compute Cloud.

entry.title: S3 — S three
entry.summary: S3 is pronounced "S three" (/ˌɛs ˈθriː/). "S-three". Simple Storage Service.

entry.title: RDS — R D S
entry.summary: RDS is pronounced "R D S" (/ˌɑːr diː ˈɛs/). Letters "R-D-S". Relational Database Service.

entry.title: IAM — I A M
entry.summary: IAM is pronounced "I A M" (/ˌaɪ eɪ ˈɛm/). Letters "I-A-M". Identity and Access Management.

entry.title: KMS — K M S
entry.summary: KMS is pronounced "K M S" (/ˌkeɪ ɛm ˈɛs/). Letters "K-M-S". Key Management Service.

entry.title: EKS — E K S
entry.summary: EKS is pronounced "E K S" (/ˌiː keɪ ˈɛs/). Letters "E-K-S". AWS managed Kubernetes.

entry.title: ECS — E C S
entry.summary: ECS is pronounced "E C S" (/ˌiː siː ˈɛs/). Letters "E-C-S". Elastic Container Service.

entry.title: GKE — G K E
entry.summary: GKE is pronounced "G K E" (/ˌdʒiː keɪ ˈiː/). Letters "G-K-E". Google Kubernetes Engine.

entry.title: AKS — A K S
entry.summary: AKS is pronounced "A K S" (/ˌeɪ keɪ ˈɛs/). Letters "A-K-S". Azure Kubernetes Service.

entry.title: Route53 — root fifty-three
entry.summary: Route53 is pronounced "root fifty-three" (/ruːt ˌfɪf ti ˈθriː/). US tech "root"; UK / general "rowt". Port 53 = DNS.

entry.title: io_uring — I O YOUR ing
entry.summary: io_uring is pronounced "I O YOUR ing" (/ˌaɪ oʊ ˈjʊər ɪŋ/). Jens Axboe: "I-O ring" (the underscore is silent). Async I/O syscall API.

entry.title: Btrfs — BUTTER F S
entry.summary: Btrfs is pronounced "BUTTER F S" (/ˈbʌt ər ɛf ɛs/). Chris Mason / kernel folks: "butter-F-S" (B-Tree File System). Letter-by-letter also common.

entry.title: ZFS — Z F S
entry.summary: ZFS is pronounced "Z F S" (/ˌziː ɛf ˈɛs/). Letters "Z-F-S". Originally Zettabyte File System.

entry.title: cgroups — C groups
entry.summary: cgroups is pronounced "C groups" (/ˈsiː ɡruːps/). "C-groups". Control groups — Linux resource isolation.

entry.title: strace — S trace
entry.summary: strace is pronounced "S trace" (/ˌɛs ˈtreɪs/). "S-trace" — system call trace.

entry.title: ltrace — L trace
entry.summary: ltrace is pronounced "L trace" (/ˌɛl ˈtreɪs/). "L-trace" — library-call trace.

entry.title: syscall — SIS call
entry.summary: syscall is pronounced "SIS call" (/ˈsɪs kɔːl/). "SIS-call". System call.

entry.title: DeepSeek-R1 — deep seek R one
entry.summary: DeepSeek-R1 is pronounced "deep seek R one" (/ˌdiːp siːk ˌɑːr ˈwʌn/). "deep-seek R-one". DeepSeek's reasoning model.

entry.title: R1 — R one
entry.summary: R1 is pronounced "R one" (/ˌɑːr ˈwʌn/). "R-one". Generic short form, usually DeepSeek-R1.

entry.title: o1 — O one
entry.summary: o1 is pronounced "O one" (/ˌoʊ ˈwʌn/). "O-one". OpenAI reasoning model.

entry.title: o3 — O three
entry.summary: o3 is pronounced "O three" (/ˌoʊ ˈθriː/). "O-three". OpenAI reasoning model.

entry.title: Sonnet — SON it
entry.summary: Sonnet is pronounced "SON it" (/ˈsɒn ɪt/). "SON-it" — like the poem. Anthropic's mid-tier Claude.

entry.title: Opus — OH pus
entry.summary: Opus is pronounced "OH pus" (/ˈoʊ pəs/). "OH-pus" — Latin for "work". Anthropic's frontier tier.

entry.title: Haiku — HIGH koo
entry.summary: Haiku is pronounced "HIGH koo" (/ˈhaɪ kuː/). "HIGH-koo" — Japanese poetic form. Anthropic's fast tier.
